zh.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch是Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot的兒子，於大約1275年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於大約1316年享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch是Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot的儿子，于大约1275年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于大约1316年享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Polish (also Bać): perhaps a variant of Bacz, from a pet form of the personal name Bartłomiej (see Bartholomew ), occuring in southeastern Poland. Aternatively, it may be a metonymic occupational name derived from baca ‘shepherd’. Compare Batz .
French: from bac ‘tub, trough’, probably applied as a metonymic occupational name or as a topographic name.

variants.name: Bartholomew
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Bertilmew, Bertelmy, a borrowing of the Old French form of the Biblical personal name Bartholomew (from Latin Bartholomaeus; Hebrew ‘son of Talmai’, said to mean ‘having many furrows’, i.e. rich in land). This was an extremely popular personal name in Christian Europe, with many vernacular derivatives. It derived its popularity from the apostle Saint Bartholomew (Matthew 10:3), who was, among other things, the patron saint of tanners, vintners, and butlers.
As an Irish name, it has been used as an Anglicized form of Mac Pharthaláin (see McFarlane ).

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Arnt
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: originally a short form of names beginning with arn ‘eagle’, for example Arnold and Arnulf .

en.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch was born about 1275, in North Brabant, Netherlands as the son of Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. He had at least 1 son. He died about 1316, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch est né environ 1275, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, fils de Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1316, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch wurde um 1275 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande, als Sohn von Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1316 im Alter von 43 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch è nato il circa 1275 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi come figlio di Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1316, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.Noord Brabant, Nederland: オランダノオルド-ブラバント
ja.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborchは約1275年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントでArnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschotの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は約1316年に43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch(은)는 대략 1275년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1316년 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.about 1275–about 1316: tany ho any amin'ny 1275–tany ho any amin'ny 1316

pt.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch nasceu aproximadamente 1275, em Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos filho de Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1316, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch родился около 1275 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды. Его отца звали Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1316 г. в возрасте 43 лет.

sm.about 1275–about 1316: e tusa o le 1275–e tusa o le 1316

es.lifeSummary: Jan Arnt Berthout Bac van Tilborch nació aproximadamente 1275, en Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos como hijo de Arnt Berthout Bac van West Tilborch tot Baeschot. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1316, a la edad de 43 años.
